title: Best 10 Herbal Remedies for Strep Throat Sufferers
intro_desc: Herbal teas for strep throat are natural remedies that help alleviate symptoms of strep throat by promoting immune function, reducing inflammation, and soothing the throat. The best herbal teas for strep throat include Echinacea purpurea, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, Echinacea angustifolia, Thymus vulgaris, Sambucus nigra, Ulmus rubra, Salvia officinalis, Achillea millefolium, and Eucalyptus globulus. If left untreated, strep throat can lead to missed work or school days, sleep disturbances, and decreased productivity, ultimately impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea has been used for many years to help soothe sore throats. This is because it contains special compounds that help to fight off infections. When you have strep throat, it's caused by a group of bacteria that can make your throat hurt and feel swollen. Echinacea purpurea tea has antiviral and antibacterial properties that can help to stop the growth of these bacteria. It also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the swelling in your throat. Drinking Echinacea purpurea tea can help to ease the pain and discomfort of strep throat, and it may also help to reduce the length of time you have to suffer from it. The tea can also help to boost your immune system, which can help your body to fight off the infection more easily.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cichoric acid is good for strep throat because it has antimicrobial properties that help fight against the bacteria that cause the infection, reducing swelling and inflammation in the throat.
Tannins, also known as peppermint, are good for strep throat because they have antibacterial properties that help reduce the growth of the Streptococcus bacteria that causes the infection.
Polyphenols, such as those found in peppermint, are good for strep throat because they have antimicrobial properties that help reduce inflammation and kill the bacteria that cause the infection.

remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for strep throat
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Mentha x piperita herbal tea that can help with strep throat, you'll need a few simple ingredients. Start by gathering fresh mint leaves, which are the leaves of the Mentha x piperita plant. You can grow your own mint plant at home or purchase it from a local store. Next, rinse the mint leaves with clean water to remove any dirt or bacteria. Then, add one tablespoon of fresh mint leaves to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. Pour in one cup of boiling water over the mint leaves, and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. You can also add a little honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to use a small amount as both can make the tea too sweet. After the tea has steeped, remove the mint leaves and drink the tea while it's still warm. Drinking Mentha x piperita tea several times a day can help reduce the swelling and pain associated with strep throat, and it's also known for its antibacterial properties that can help fight off the infection.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, also known as licorice root tea, is good for strep throat because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce swelling and pain in the throat. The tea also contains compounds that fight infection, which helps to combat the bacteria that cause strep throat. Additionally, licorice root tea can help soothe a sore throat, making it easier to swallow and talk. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties may also help reduce fever, which is a common symptom of strep throat. Some people also find that licorice root tea helps calm a cough, making it a popular natural remedy for strep throat.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin (peppermint) is good for strep throat because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and fight off the infection caused by the streptococcus bacteria.
Polyphenols, found in peppermint, are good for strep throat because they have antibacterial properties that help reduce the growth of the bacteria that cause the infection and ease its symptoms.
Tannins, also known as peppermint oil, are good for strep throat because they have antibacterial properties that can help kill the Streptococcus bacteria that cause the infection.

remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for strep throat
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Mentha x piperita herbal tea that can help with strep throat, start by gathering fresh or dried peppermint leaves. You can either purchase peppermint leaves at a local health food store or grow your own in your garden. If using fresh leaves, simply chop them into small pieces and add 1 tablespoon to a teapot or a large mug. If using dried leaves, use 1 teaspoon. Next, boil water and pour it over the peppermint leaves, letting it steep for 5-7 minutes. The longer it steeps, the stronger the flavor and the more benefits you'll get. Once the tea has steeped, strain the leaves and add honey to taste - this will help soothe your throat. You can drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce inflammation, ease pain, and fight off the infection that's causing your strep throat.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, is a natural remedy for strep throat. This tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the throat. Ginger contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which are known to fight against the bacteria that cause strep throat. Drinking ginger tea can also help soothe the throat, reducing discomfort and making it easier to swallow. Additionally, ginger tea has antiviral properties, which can help prevent the spread of infection. The anti-inflammatory properties in ginger tea can also help reduce fever, which is often associated with strep throat. Drinking ginger tea regularly may also boost the immune system, helping the body fight off the infection more efficiently.

remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for strep throat
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Mentha x piperita herbal tea, which helps with strep throat, start by gathering a handful of fresh peppermint leaves. You can also use dried peppermint leaves if fresh ones are not available. First, boil some water in a pot and then reduce the heat to a simmer. Add a handful of peppermint leaves to the pot and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. You can also let it steep for 10-15 minutes for a stronger flavor. Once the tea is ready, strain it into a cup using a strainer or a tea infuser to remove the peppermint leaves. You can add honey to the tea if you want it sweeter and drink it while it's still warm. Drink 2-3 cups of peppermint tea a day to help soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation. Peppermint tea has antibacterial properties that can help combat the bacteria that cause strep throat, and its anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce the swelling and pain in the throat.

These antioxidants and flavonoids help reduce inflammation and kill the bacteria that cause strep throat, such as Group A Streptococcus. Sambucus nigra tea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe a sore throat and reduce pain. When you drink the tea, the active compounds can reach your throat and start working to heal the infection. Additionally, the tea can help boost your immune system, which can help your body fight off the infection faster. Drinking Sambucus nigra tea may help reduce the duration and severity of strep throat symptoms. The tea can be consumed hot or cold, sweetened with honey if desired, and is a gentle way to help your body recover from strep throat.

Next, you'll need a tea infuser or a metal strainer, and a pot to boil water in. Now, add a handful of fresh peppermint leaves to your tea infuser or strainer and place it in the pot of boiling water. remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, has been used for centuries to help fight off infections like strep throat. The antibacterial properties in thyme make it effective against the bacteria that cause strep throat. When you drink thyme tea, the active ingredients in the plant go into your body and start working to kill the bacteria. This can help bring down the swelling and reduce the pain and soreness in your throat. Thyme also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the redness and discomfort in your throat. Additionally, thyme has a natural antiseptic property that helps to clean and protect the affected area. By drinking thyme tea, you can help your body fight off the infection and start feeling better.

When you drink slippery elm tea, these mucilages can help calm inflammation and reduce irritation in the throat. This can make it easier to swallow and speak, and may even help alleviate some of the pain associated with strep throat. Additionally, slippery elm tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and promote healing in the throat. Some people also use slippery elm tea to help loosen and clear mucus from the throat, which can aid in recovery from strep throat.

remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal teas for strep throat
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Mentha x piperita herbal tea that can help with strep throat, start by gathering fresh peppermint leaves. Choose healthy-looking leaves with no signs of wilting or damage. Next, rinse the leaves with clean water to remove any dirt or impurities. In a saucepan, combine 1 cup of water and 1/4 cup of fresh peppermint leaves. Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer for 5-7 minutes. Allow the peppermint leaves to steep in the water, releasing their natural oils and flavor. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, pressing on the solids to extract as much liquid as possible. Discard the solids and add honey to taste, if desired. Let the tea cool slightly before drinking. Peppermint tea has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe a sore throat and reduce pain, making it a popular remedy for strep throat.

supplementary_best: A combination of Echinacea purpurea, Thymus vulgaris, and Eucalyptus globulus is effective in treating strep throat. Echinacea purpurea is an immune system booster, which helps fight the infection. Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, has antibacterial properties that directly target and kill the bacteria causing strep throat. Eucalyptus globulus, or eucalyptus, has decongestant properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the throat. This combination works well because it addresses the infection from multiple angles: it boosts the immune system, targets the bacteria, and eases symptoms.
supplementary_avoid: If you have strep throat, it's best to avoid certain herbal teas that might make your condition worse. Some examples include teas made from 'Yarrow' (Achillea millefolium-free) alternative herbs like 'Rat tail plant' (Selinum carvifolium), as these plants belong to the same plant family as ragweed and may trigger allergic reactions or worsen respiratory issues. Another herb to avoid is 'Comfrey' (Symphytum officinale), which can cause stomach problems and interact with medications. 'Licorice root' (Glycyrrhiza glabra-free) alternative like 'Peruvian maca' (Lepidium meyenii) is also not recommended, as it can lower blood pressure and interact with blood thinners. 'Slippery elm' (Ulmus rubra-free) alternative like 'Marshmallow root' (Althaea officinalis) can cause stomach problems and interact with blood pressure medications. Lastly, 'Licorice root' (Glycyrrhiza glabra-free) alternative like 'Dandelion root' (Taraxacum officinale) may cause stomach problems and interact with blood thinners. If you have a severe case of strep throat, it's best to consult your doctor before trying any herbal remedies.
supplementary_other_remedies: When you have strep throat, your throat and tonsils get infected, causing pain and swelling. To help ease the discomfort and speed up the healing process, you can try some natural remedies besides herbal teas. For example, gargling with saltwater can help kill bacteria and reduce swelling. The salt helps to pull out the excess mucus and bacteria from your throat, giving you some relief from the pain. Another option is to use a humidifier to add moisture to the air, which can help soothe an itchy and sore throat. The added moisture can also help loosen up mucus and make it easier to cough up, allowing your body to get rid of the infection faster. Some people also find it helpful to try warm compresses or a warm bath to relax their throat and loosen up the mucus. This can help reduce the pain and discomfort, making it easier to rest and recover from the illness. Additionally, some people swear by the healing properties of honey, which can help soothe a sore throat and provide some antibacterial properties to help fight off the infection.
supplementary_related_ailments: When people have strep throat, they often feel weak and tired. This is because their body is fighting the infection. Some people may also experience headaches, body aches, and fever. This is because the infection is causing inflammation in their body. To help with these symptoms, some medicinal herbs can be used. For example, ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce body aches and fever. It can also help with digestion, which can be affected when a person has strep throat. Echinacea is another herb that can help boost the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off the infection. This can help reduce the severity of the symptoms, such as the sore throat. Yarrow has antiseptic properties that can help reduce swelling and fight off the infection. It can also help with fever and body aches. Finally, licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe a sore throat. It can also help with digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the body.  Other symptoms that people with strep throat may experience include a loss of appetite, nausea, and vomiting. These symptoms can be caused by the infection affecting the digestive system. To help with these symptoms, some medicinal herbs can be used. For example, peppermint has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe nausea and vomiting. It can also help with digestion and reduce inflammation in the body. Slippery elm has a soothing effect on the digestive system, which can help reduce nausea and vomiting. It can also help with digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the body. Finally, chamomile has a calming effect on the body, which can help reduce nausea and vomiting. It can also help with digestive issues and promote relaxation.  Some people with strep throat may also experience earaches and sinus pressure. These symptoms can be caused by the infection affecting the ears and sinuses. To help with these symptoms, some medicinal herbs can be used. For example, eucalyptus has decongestant properties that can help reduce sinus pressure. It can also help with respiratory issues and promote relaxation. Echinacea is another herb that can help boost the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off the infection. This can help reduce the severity of the symptoms, such as earaches and sinus pressure. Finally, mullein has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce earaches and sinus pressure. It can also help with respiratory issues and promote relaxation.  It's worth noting that while medicinal herbs can help alleviate symptoms, they should not be used as a replacement for medical treatment. If you have strep throat, it's still important to see a doctor and follow their recommended treatment plan.
